title: Getting Started
content:
### Setting Up a React Project with Vite Vite is the recommended build tool for new React projects. It provides fast development servers and optimized production builds. --- ### Creating a New Project ```bash npm create vite@latest my-app -- --template react cd my-app npm install npm run dev ``` This scaffolds a React project with: - A `src/` directory for source code - `public/` for static assets - `vite.config.js` for build configuration - `index.html` as the entry point ### Project Structure | File/Directory | Purpose | |----------------|---------| | `src/main.jsx` | Application entry point | | `src/App.jsx` | Root component | | `src/App.css` | Root component styles | | `index.html` | HTML template | | `vite.config.js` | Vite configuration | ### Your First Component Create `src/App.jsx` with a simple component: ```jsx function App() { return (

Hello, React!

); } export default App; ``` > **Key Point:** Always run `npm install` after creating a project to install dependencies. Use `npm run dev` for development and `npm run build` for production.
codeExample:
npm create vite@latest my-app -- --template react
cd my-app
npm install
npm run dev

quiz.question: Which build tool is recommended for new React projects?
What command starts the development server in a Vite React project?
